what_it_does: Decompresses and expands a compact user transmission into a structured design brief for a company-internal noticeboard and request system, mapping it onto existing channel, registry, recall, and autonomous-build primitives.
observations: The document declares itself to be the first `idea` item on the board it describes.
It proposes typed board items — request, issue, tip, guide, idea/thought — each with a lifecycle.
It identifies four candidate registries: item-type, source-type, channel-attachment, and channel/Space.
It frames GitHub history and Claude Code transcripts as correlatable source types joined by author, path, and time.
It generalizes channel attachments so channels and Spaces can be parametrically generated from registry rows.
It proposes an autonomous sweep routine, manual or scheduled, that triages and builds from the board.
It connects the idea to framework concepts: gap-pressure, introspective data-building, the Heart, and channel-fork.
It includes a phased build plan and leaves the final naming open.
standouts: The noticeboard is positioned as the inward-facing half of the channel system, making Company self-talk a first-class artifact.
The entire design is framed as registry rows and typed edges rather than bespoke code.
The file is self-referential: it is captured as the first `idea` item on the board it specifies.
It proposes pointing the existing autonomous build loop at a request board as its work queue.
conventions: Follows the channel-memory frontmatter convention with trust, author, date, verified, and relates fields.
Uses explicit `Tim-direct` and `lead` labels to separate user content from framing connections.
Uses numbered sections, tables, and cross-references to related project files.
Breaks typical compact note style by intentionally stretching a single transmission into a long-form build brief.
concerns: The heavy decompression makes it hard to tell which ideas are Tim's and which are the lead's interpretive connections.
Several registry mechanisms are asserted as already existing or mostly existing without file-local evidence.
Item schema, lifecycle rules, and triage policy are proposed but not finalized.
The channel-fork/rollback analogy is captured as a real primitive though its implementation status is unclear.
The open naming suggests the concept is still unstable.
notes: A design-seed document that turns a user's spoken idea for an internal request board into a registry-driven build plan tied to the Company's existing channel and recall fabric.
questions: Which channel or Space will initially host the noticeboard?
What is the exact schema and required fields for a board item?
How will the autonomous sweep decide which items are buildable and in what order?
Is channel-fork/rollback already implemented or still a proposed primitive?
How will the GitHub source-adapter correlate commits with Claude Code transcripts in practice?
Who besides the lead is authorized to pick up and resolve items?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a faithful decompression of Tim's compacted message into a build-ready shape, and the content does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Introspective tooling — the Company observing and improving itself.
Registry-driven, parametric architecture where new types and attachments require no code changes.
Fractal reuse of existing channel, recall, and build-loop primitives.
Making gap-pressure explicit and actionable through authored requests and issues.
Self-documenting origin stories and self-referential seed items.
intention_signals: To convert an informal user request into a concrete, buildable system design that the fabric can discuss, refine, and execute, while anchoring every new mechanism to existing channel, registry, and recall primitives.
novelty: It treats an internal request/issue/idea board not as a separate product but as the inward-facing projection of the same channel-and-registry substrate, and it makes the design document itself the first item on that board.
contribution: A structured seed brief that names the noticeboard concept, defines candidate item types and lifecycles, identifies the registries needed, and proposes a phased build sequence the fabric can react to and execute.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory vision document that decompresses a user transmission into a design for a Company-internal noticeboard and request system, specifying typed items, registries, recall integration, an autonomous sweep routine, and a build plan.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and structure the user's idea for an inward-facing request, issue, and idea board so the fabric can review, refine, and begin building it through existing channel and registry primitives.
